observedGaps: No code-owned Socrates surface registry or generated surface map existed.
Identity core was not guaranteed in the stable cache prelude.
User and project metadata were embedded in the system string, invalidating the stable prefix across projects.
Chat attachments were image-only, capped at 12, and allowed 15 MB per image without a combined submission cap.
Inline chat content had no 10,000-character backend cap or automatic large-paste attachment path.
Compaction accepted anchors that matched syntax without proving the referenced turn was in compressor input.
Any token reduction was accepted; no 100-120k post-compaction target or 180k provider hard stop was enforced.
Existing active snapshots did not remove their already-compacted raw turns before later model calls.
GPT-5.6 Luna and Tencent HY 3 were absent from the model catalog; GLM 5.2 thinking controls were stale.
